Capability map (illustrative)

The market sells pieces. SourceA is the full row.

Builders wire workflows. Orchestrators move tasks. Governance slides promise policy. This map shows which capabilities each approach typically covers — not a scored competitive audit.

ApproachBuildPolicyReceiptReplayDFYWeekly proof
Freelance agent builder●●
n8n / Zapier automation●●
Temporal / LangGraph (partner pattern — not substitute)●●
Governance consulting / slides●
SourceA — reference standard

Your orchestrator underneath. Signed receipts on top. Temporal and LangGraph are partner patterns — not substitutes for a signed receipt.

Illustrative capability map only — not certification, legal advice, or a competitive scorecard. Methodology: EXTERNAL_DESIGN_BENCHMARK_2026-06-15_v1.md (SourceA archive).

Chat Unify ConnectCursor WorkerTemporalLangGraphn8nsourcea-bootMCP verifyHosted webhook

SourceA ships Chat Unify.app with Connect hub — Cursor, webhooks, and n8n templates — plus governed receipts n8n/Zapier alone do not provide. Integration docs →

◇Start in shadowProve policy fires correctly before you enforce in production.
◇Evidence, not chatReceipts on your disk — durable for counsel and clients.
◇Fail closedBLOCK stops the loop. Tamper surfaces if someone alters a record.
◇Yours to keepSelf-hosted policy — no vendor holds your audit trail hostage.

Pick your starting point

Audit first, build next, or retain — fixed bands, no hourly surprise.
